member.name: T:GameHelper.Cache.DisappearingEntity
member.summary:
The purpose of this class is to solve the disappearing entity problem. Basically, there are a group of entities that disappears automatically after the environment is gone from the map. The problem happens when those disappearing entities are outside the network bubble, GameHelper will never know to clean them up.
For example, when breach is opened, breach environment is activated, and all the breach entities start spawning. Once breach is completed/closed, all of the spawned breach entities disappears (even if they are not in the network bubble). However, GameHelper still show them and process them as they are still there.
Disappearing entity problem does not impact all the environments e.g. (Legion) because in that case 99.9% of the time the entities that disappear will be in the network bubble. So this solution will be applied on case by case basis.
NOTE: Currently activated environment information is available in structure and environment key details can be read from Environment.dat GGPK file.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: A string in the entity path that helps identify the entity belonging to this cache.
Environment keys minimum value to activate this cache.
Environment keys maximum value to activate this cache.
Reference to the structure storing all the entities known to GameHelper.

member.name: T:GameHelper.GameProcess
member.summary: Allows process manipulation. It uses the (time/event based) co-routines to continuously monitor and open a process with the specific name. It exposes variables/events for the caller to use. Base class OnControllerReady is only triggered when all static addresses are found. Limitation: This class will not open a game process if multiple processes match the name because it does not know which process to select.

member.name: F:GameHelper.RemoteEnums.Entity.EntityStates.Useless
member.summary:
A special entity state that will make sure that this entity components are never updated again from the game memory. This is to save the CPU cycles on such entity. All plugins are expected to skip this entity. All can have this state.
WARNING: if an entity reaches this state it can never go back to not-useless unless current area/zone changes.

members.member:
Stats enum taken from GGPK file -> Stats.dat -> Id Column and made c# friendly via the sed bash command.
NOTE: sed commad -> sed 's/+/positive_/g; s/%/percentage/g; s/-/negative_/g; s/$/,/g;' NOTE: Do not forget to add '= 1' on the first enum. NOTE: flask_charges_used_+% whatever you read in _rid, add +1 to it. NOTE: offset finder tool now have this value -> Charges group

member.summary: Gets the details of all known Active Skills.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating if the skill can be used or not.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.MinionCommandSkills
member.summary: Gets the "command minion" skills available on this player's summoned minions, keyed by skill name (e.g. "KnifeThrow"). The value is true when the command is usable on at least one summoned minion (i.e. that minion has it off cooldown). These commands carry their cooldown on the minion, not the player, so they never appear in . Only populated for the player's own actor.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.UsableMinionCommandSkills
member.summary: Gets the names of that are currently usable on at least one summoned minion. Parallels for minion commands.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.DeployedEntities
member.summary: Gets the number of entities deployed by this entity, keyed by DeployedObjectType id. Index it with a type id; absent ids read as 0. PoE2 uses large (dat-row) type ids, so this is dictionary-backed rather than a fixed array.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.ToImGui
member.summary: Converts the class data to ImGui.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.UpdateMinionCommandSkills(GameOffsets.Objects.Components.DeployedEntityStructure[])
member.summary: Aggregates the "command minion" skills off the player's summoned minions into / . Only the player's own actor scans (gated on the owner being the current player); other actors just clear their (empty) collections cheaply.
member.param: the deployed-entity array already read for this actor.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Actor.CollectCommandSkills(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Reads this (minion) actor's "command minion" skills (keys in the 0x40000000 range) and folds them into by name, OR-ing usability so a command counts as usable when any minion has it off cooldown.
member.param: player-level name -> usable map to add to.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Base
member.summary: The component in the entity. Present on item entities, it exposes the item's base type: the rendered (localized) display name and the internal, locale-independent BaseItemTypes id.
member.remarks: Reading the display name straight from memory means callers no longer need the user to copy an item to the clipboard to learn its name (the old RitualHelper workflow). It also disambiguates currency/rune/essence TIERS that share one art.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Base.#ctor(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: address of the component.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Base.BaseItemName
member.summary: Gets the rendered, localized base-type display name (e.g. "Greater Orb of Augmentation"). The price-lookup key for non-unique items. Empty when unavailable.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.Base.InternalName
member.summary: Gets the internal, locale-independent BaseItemTypes id (e.g. "CurrencyAddModToMagic2"). A stable key for matching across languages/patches. Empty when unavailable.

member.summary: Converts the class data to ImGui.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.DeployedObjectCounter
member.summary: Counts deployed objects per object-type id. PoE2 changed the deployed-object "type" field from a small (0-255) enum into a full dat-row id (e.g. 22938), so a fixed int[256] can no longer hold it. This is backed by a dictionary instead, and the indexer returns 0 for ids that are not currently deployed — preserving the old "absent == 0, never throws" semantics that AutoHotKeyTrigger's DeployedObjectsCount[id] dynamic conditions rely on.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.DeployedObjectCounter.CategoryName(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the human-readable category name for a DeployedObjectType id, or "NO NAME MAPPING" when the id is not in yet.
member.param: the deployed-object type id.
member.returns: the category name, or "NO NAME MAPPING".

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.DeployedObjectCounter.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the number of deployed objects of the given type id (0 if none are deployed).
member.param: the deployed-object type id (see DV -> Actor -> Deployed Objects).

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.DeployedObjectCounter.Clear
member.summary: Removes all counts. Called at the start of each actor update.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.DeployedObjectCounter.Increment(System.Int32)
member.summary: Increments the count for the given type id.
member.param: the deployed-object type id.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.RenderItem
member.summary: The component in the entity. Present on item entities, it exposes the item's 2D inventory art (.dds) path.
member.remarks: The art basename is a stable, unambiguous identity for uniques (each unique has its own icon) and matches poe.ninja / poe2scout's IconUrl basename, so it can be used as a price-lookup key without reading the unique's name. Currency tiers share one art, so prefer the name for non-uniques.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.Components.StateMachine.TryGetRuneStationSocketCount(System.Int32@)
member.summary: Attempts to read the authoritative socket/hole count from the RuneStation object that listens on this StateMachine. Walks the listener vector at SM + 0x20, resolves each listener back to its station (station = *(node) - 0x98), and verifies the station's device back-pointer (station + 0x10) matches this component's owner entity before reading the count at station + 0x38. Works out of the network bubble since the station persists. Callers should fall back to the "sockets" state if this returns false.
member.param: The resolved socket/hole count, when found.
member.returns: True if a matching RuneStation was resolved; otherwise false.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase
member.summary: Points to a Memory location and reads/understands all the data from there. CurrentAreaInstance in remote memory location changes w.r.t time or event. Due to this, each remote memory object requires to implement a time/event based coroutine.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.#ctor(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: address of the remote memory object.
True in case the object should be updated even if address hasn't changed.

Pass true if derived object takes the responsibility of executing the in constructor. This fixes the issue where derived object is introducing a new dependency and that dependency isn't initialized until the derived object constructor is executed.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.Address
member.summary: Gets or sets the address of the memory location.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.ToImGui
member.summary: Converts the to ImGui Widget via reflection. By default, only knows how to convert field and properties of the calling class. For details on which specific properties are ignored read method description. Any other properties or fields of the derived class should be handled by that class.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.UpdateData(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Reads the memory and update all the data known by this Object.
member.param: true in case the address has changed; otherwise false.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.CleanUpData
member.summary: Knows how to clean up the object.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.GetToImGuiMethods(System.Type,System.Reflection.BindingFlags,System.Object)
member.summary: Iterates over properties of the given class via reflection and yields the property name and its method. Any property that doesn't have both the getter and setter method are ignored.
member.param: Type of the class to traverse.
flags to filter the class properties.
Class object, or null for static class.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.RemoteObjectBase.SkipImGuiReflection
member.summary: Attribute to put on the properties that you want to skip in method.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AreaInstance.ScanSleepingEntities(System.Func{System.String,System.Boolean},System.Action{GameOffsets.Objects.States.InGameState.EntityNodeKey,GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.Entity})
member.summary: Scans the game's SleepingEntities memory map (decorations / out-of-bubble objects) and invokes for each entity whose path satisfies . Sleeping entities exist at a larger radius than awake ones, so this can surface objects beyond the network bubble. NOTE: the callback runs in parallel (see ), so and anything it writes to must be thread-safe.
member.param: Predicate on the entity path deciding which entities to keep.
Invoked (possibly concurrently) for each matching entity.
member.returns: Total number of sleeping entities scanned.

member.summary: Snapshot of one endgame Atlas map node exposed for plugins.

member.summary: Gets the child index under .

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.Address
member.summary: Gets the underlying Atlas node UiElement address.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.MapId
member.summary: Gets the internal map id/name read from the Atlas node metadata.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.Name
member.summary: Gets the internal map id/name read from the Atlas node metadata.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.DisplayName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable in-game name resolved from , falling back to the raw id when no mapping is known.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.Type
member.summary: Gets the map type classification ("normal" or "unique"), resolved from via the embedded tags database.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.Tags
member.summary: Gets feature tags (e.g. "lineage", "arbiter") for this map, resolved from via the embedded tags database.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.GridPosition
member.summary: Gets the node's Atlas grid position.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.BiomeId
member.summary: Gets the biome id read from the Atlas node metadata.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.State
member.summary: Gets the discovered completion/accessibility state.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.ContentNames
member.summary: Gets raw content/badge names attached to this node, when known.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.BadgeAddresses
member.summary: Gets badge UiElement addresses under Atlas node child path [0][0].

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.BadgeCount
member.summary: Gets the number of badge UiElements under Atlas node child path [0][0].

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.ContentTokens
member.summary: Gets the raw per-node content tokens (class-1 content: the StdVector<u32> living on the Atlas node UiElement at element+0x350). Populated only for visible/rendered nodes. Resolution of a token to a content name is left to consumers.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.BadgeContentIds
member.summary: Gets the raw class-2 (badge) content ids — the u32 at badge+0x188 of each node[0][0] child. The high word is a constant category; the content type is the low 16 bits. Resolution of an id to a content name is left to consumers.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.ConnectedGridPositions
member.summary: Gets connected Atlas grid positions read from the Atlas connection data, when available.

member.name: F:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.ContentTokenEffects
member.summary: Known effect-token id (low 16 bits of a value in ) → effect-text template. A "{0}" placeholder is replaced with the token's magnitude (high16 / 64) at resolve time. Unmapped ids have no entry (callers fall back to the raw hex value).

member.name: F:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.BadgeContentNames
member.summary: Known content id (low 16 bits of a value in ) → named-content title (the bold tooltip header). Unmapped ids have no entry (callers fall back to hex).

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.GetContentTokenName(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Resolves a content token to its effect text, or null when the token is unmapped. The token's low 16 bits select the effect; a "{0}" in the template is replaced with the magnitude (high 16 bits / 64).
member.param: raw content-token value (see ).
member.returns: the resolved effect text, or null if the effect id is unknown.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.GetBadgeContentName(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Resolves a badge content id to its named-content title, or null when unmapped. Keys on the low 16 bits (the high word is the constant 0x0002 category tag).
member.param: raw badge-content value (see ).
member.returns: the resolved content title, or null if the content id is unknown.

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.AtlasMapNode.GetContentDisplayNames(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns this node's content as a merged, de-duplicated display list: each badge resolves to its content title and each token to its effect text (raw hex when unmapped). Titles (from ) come first, then the effect lines (from ), mirroring the in-game tooltip; duplicates are removed.
member.param: when true, values with no known name are included as their raw hex (debug view); when false, only resolved (mapped) names are returned.
member.returns: the merged display names, badge-titles-then-token-effects, with duplicates removed.

member.summary: Observable Atlas map node states.

member.name: T:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements
member.summary: This is actually UiRoot main child which contains all the UiElements (100+). Normally it's at index 1 of UiRoot. This class is created because traversing childrens of UiRoot is a slow process that requires lots of memory reads. Drawback: 1: Every league/patch the offsets needs to be updated. Offsets used over here are very unstable. 2: More UiElements we are tracking = More memory read every X seconds just to update IsVisible :(.

member.name: F:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.passiveskilltreenodes
member.summary: Passive skill tree node Parent UI element. UiRoot -> MainChild -> index 28 -> 1

member.name: F:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.sekhemasTrialMapPanel
member.summary: Sekhemas Trial Map panel. UiRoot MainChild -> child 1 -> child 84 -> child 0

member.name: M:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.#ctor(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: UiRoot 1st child address (starting from 0) or in case UiRoot has no child.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.LargeMap
member.summary: Gets the LargeMap UiElement. UiRoot -> MainChild -> 3rd index -> 1nd index.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.MiniMap
member.summary: Gets the MiniMap UiElement. UiRoot -> MainChild -> 3rd index -> 2nd index.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.WorldMapPanel
member.summary: Gets the checkpoint / world-travel map panel UiElement. It is only while that screen is open (opened by interacting with a checkpoint). The in-area LargeMap stays visible underneath it, so consumers gate on this to tell the two apart. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Act1
member.summary: Gets the Act 1 tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 0.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Act2
member.summary: Gets the Act 2 tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 1.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Act3
member.summary: Gets the Act 3 tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 2.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Act4
member.summary: Gets the Act 4 tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 3.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Interlude
member.summary: Gets the Interlude tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 5.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.Atlas
member.summary: Gets the Atlas tab under the checkpoint / world-travel map panel. GameUi -> child 22 -> child 0 -> child 6.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.AtlasMaps
member.summary: Gets the current Atlas map nodes exposed for plugins.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.AtlasMarkers
member.summary: Gets the "you are here" marker children on the Atlas (fp 0x502EF3). These are not real map nodes — they render on the player's current node. Exposed so plugins can draw a position indicator.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.LeftPanel
member.summary: Gets the currently-open left-side panel UiElement (character, skills, etc.). It is only while such a panel is open; the backing pointer is null when no left panel is open. UiRoot manager -> 0x6D8.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.RightPanel
member.summary: Gets the currently-open right-side panel UiElement (inventory, vendor/shop, stash, etc.). It is only while such a panel is open; the backing pointer is null when no right panel is open. UiRoot manager -> 0x6E0.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.IsAnyLargePanelOpen
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether any large blocking panel is currently open (a left/right side panel, the passive skill tree, or the world-travel map). Useful for overlays that should hide world-space drawing while the player is in a menu.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.IsPassiveSkillTreeOpen
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the passive skill tree is currently open. Gated on the visibility of the tree's node container (UiRoot manager -> 0x730 -> child 2), not on : in v0.5.x the per-node SkillInfo pointer reads null so that list never populates, but the container's visibility bit is reliable.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.SekhemasTrialMapPanel
member.summary: Gets the Sekhemas Trial Map panel UiElement. Visible only during Trial of the Sekhemas. UiRoot MainChild -> child 1 -> child 84 -> child 0.

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.ChatParent
member.summary: Gets the Chat UiElement parent. UiRoot -> MainChild -> this index keeps moving around

member.name: P:GameHelper.RemoteObjects.States.InGameStateObjects.ImportantUiElements.SkillTreeNodesUiElements
member.summary: Gets the skill tree nodes UI Elements. UiRoot -> MainChild -> index 28 -> 2 -> all childrens;

member.name: T:GameHelper.Ui.MemoryReadDiagnostics
member.summary:
Diagnostic window that aggregates failed process-memory reads so a human can tell whether they are transient torn reads (the game mutating data while we read it) or a real offset/struct-layout breakage.
The decisive signal is per-address recurrence: a torn read lands on whatever happened to be in freed/reallocated memory, so failures spread across many distinct addresses each seen only once or twice. A wrong offset reads the same bad field every frame, so a small number of addresses rack up huge repeat counts. Compare the "Unique" and "Max/Addr" columns: high Unique + low Max/Addr ⇒ races; low Unique + high Max/Addr ⇒ likely structural.
Recording is gated on and costs nothing (no stack walk, no bookkeeping) when the window is closed.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Ui.MemoryReadDiagnostics.InitializeCoroutines
member.summary: Starts the window render coroutine.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Ui.MemoryReadDiagnostics.RecordFailure(System.String,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Records a failed memory read. Callers must check before computing the (relatively expensive) caller string, so this method assumes recording is wanted.
member.param: name of the type that failed to read.
"Assembly!Type.Method" of the originating call site.
the address that failed to read.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Ui.MemoryReadDiagnostics.DecodeHint(System.Int64)
member.summary: Renders a failing value's plausible interpretations (UTF-16 / ASCII text, or a float/double) so a human can recognise it as data rather than a pointer. Returns an empty string when nothing decodes cleanly.
member.param: the raw 64-bit value that was (mis)used as an address.

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.MathHelper
member.summary: Utility functions to help with some math.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.MathHelper.Lerp(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Linearly interpolates between two points. Interpolates between the points a and b by the interpolant t. The parameter t is clamped to the range[0, 1]. This is most commonly used to find a point some fraction of the way along a line between two endpoints (e.g.to move an object gradually between those points). The value returned equals a + (b - a) * t (which can also be written a * (1-t) + b*t). When t = 0, Lerp(a, b, t) returns a. When t = 1, Lerp(a, b, t) returns b. When t = 0.5, Lerp(a, b, t) returns the point midway between a and b.
member.param: Starting point.
Destination point.
Interpolation ratio.
member.returns: Interpolated value, equals to a + (b - a) * t.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.MathHelper.Lerp(System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Single)
member.summary: Linearly interpolates between two vectors. Interpolates between the vectors a and b by the interpolant t. The parameter t is clamped to the range[0, 1]. This is most commonly used to find a point some fraction of the way along a line between two endpoints (e.g.to move an object gradually between those points). Similar to .
member.param: Starting point.
Destination point.
Interpolation ratio.
member.returns: Interpolated value, equals to a + (b - a) * t.

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder
member.summary: This class contains helper functions to find the patterns (array of bytes in HEX) in the process memory. To improve the perforamnce and memory footprint, it parallelizes the search and ensures that the whole executable is not loaded in the memory at once. NOTE: According to microsoft docs (linked below) anything bigger than 85,000 bytes will go into the large-object-heap and will remain in the memory for a long time. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/garbage-collection/large-object-heap.

member.name: F:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder.MaxBytesObject
member.summary: This is 1000 less than the maximum number of bytes that can be created with non-large-object-heap. Benefit of doing that is to ensure that GC cleans up the memory ASAP. NOTE: 1000 less than the maximum number allows us to read a bit more than this number when require.

member.name: P:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder.Patterns
member.summary: Gets the HEX (byte array) patterns for finding static offsets in the Process. All patterns are read from the GameOffsets library so that users just have to update GameOffsets lib once there is a new patch.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder.Find(GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to find all the patterns given in the GameOffsets StaticOffsetsPatterns class.
member.param: Handle to the process.
BaseAddress of the process main module.
Total Size of the process main module.
member.returns: Static offsets name and location in the processs.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder.BiggestPatternLength
member.summary: Gets the length of the biggest pattern.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.PatternFinder.CalculateTotalReadOperations(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the total number of read operations required for a given process size based on the MaxBytesObject constant.
member.param: Size of the process main module.
member.returns: total number of read operations requried.

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle
member.summary: Handle to a process.

member.name: F:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.MaxUserModeAddress
member.summary: Max valid user-mode address on 64-bit Windows (48-bit addressing).

member.name: F:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.MinValidAddress
member.summary: Lowest address that can ever back a real allocation. The first 64 KiB is the reserved null-pointer partition on Windows x64, and allocation granularity is 64 KiB, so anything below this is never a valid pointer. Game pointers live far above this (module base 0x140000000+, heap in the TiB range), so this floor never rejects a legitimate read — it only catches data values (floats, small ints, string fragments) that a torn read fed in where a pointer was expected.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.#ctor
member.summary: Required by SafeHandle infrastructure for finalizer / marshaling support. Private to prevent callers accidentally constructing a zombie handle without a PID — see audit F-034. Real construction must go through the ctor.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: processId you want to access.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.ReadMemory``1(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Reads the process memory as type T.
member.typeparam: type of data structure to read.
member.param: address to read the data from.
member.returns: data from the process in T format.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.TryReadMemory``1(System.IntPtr,``0@)
member.summary: Reads the process memory as type T without logging on failure. Returns false (and = default) when the handle is invalid, the address fails the sanity check, or the underlying read fails. Use this on hot, inherently-racy paths (e.g. walking a live, concurrently-mutated container) where torn reads are expected and recoverable, so they don't flood the log. Use elsewhere so a genuine offset/layout breakage still surfaces.
member.typeparam: type of data structure to read.
member.param: address to read the data from.
data read from the process, or default on failure.
member.returns: true if the read succeeded; otherwise false.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.RecordDiagnosticFailure(System.String,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Records a failed read into the diagnostics window when it is enabled. Gated up front so the (stack-walking) caller lookup is never paid in normal operation.
member.param: name of the type that failed to read.
the address that failed.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.IsValidAddress(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Cheap sanity check that an address could plausibly back a real allocation in the target process. Rejects the reserved low memory range and addresses beyond the 48-bit user-mode limit. Does not guarantee the address is currently mapped.
member.param: address to check.
member.returns: true if the address is within the plausible user-mode range.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.DescribeCaller
member.summary: Walks the current call stack to find the first frame outside this class and returns "AssemblyName!Type.Method". Used only on the (rare) memory-read error path to attribute a bad read to core vs. a specific plugin assembly. The assembly name distinguishes plugins (each loaded under its own ALC/name) from GameHelper core. Stack capture is skipped for line info to keep it cheap.
member.returns: caller description, or a fallback string if it can't be determined.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.SafeMemoryHandle.ReleaseHandle
member.summary: When overridden in a derived class, executes the code required to free the handle.
member.returns: true if the handle is released successfully; otherwise, in the event of a catastrophic failure, false. In this case, it generates a releaseHandleFailed MDA Managed Debugging Assistant.

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.UniversalFont
member.summary:
Loads a bundled "universal" font into the overlay so text in ANY language renders across the whole overlay (every plugin draws through the shared ImGui atlas, so they all benefit).
One merged font is built (first source that has a glyph wins; later sources only fill gaps): 1. DejaVuSans — pretty Latin + Cyrillic + Greek (priority for those). 2. the configured GH font () — keeps the user's language pretty (e.g. CJK), merged so it only fills DejaVu's gaps. 3. GNU Unifont — fallback over the whole BMP (CJK/Arabic/Hebrew/Thai/Armenian/…).
Gated by ; when off, the normal configured font is loaded instead. Call on startup and whenever the font settings change.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.UniversalFont.ApplyFromSettings
member.summary: Loads the universal font when is set, otherwise the normally-configured font. Safe to call before the overlay exists.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.UniversalFont.ApplyConfigured
member.summary: Loads the normally-configured GH font (honouring the custom glyph range when set).

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaNames
member.summary:
Maps internal WorldArea ids (e.g. "MapHiddenGrotto", "G1_1") to their human-readable in-game names (e.g. "Hidden Grotto", "The Riverbank").
The mapping is loaded once from the embedded Data/WorldAreaNames.tsv resource. To refresh it after a game patch, regenerate that file and rebuild.

member.name: P:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaNames.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of known id -> name mappings.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaNames.GetDisplayName(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the human-readable name for an internal WorldArea id, or the id itself when no mapping is known (so callers always get a usable string).
member.param: internal WorldArea id read from game memory.

member.name: M:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaNames.TryGetDisplayName(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to get the human-readable name for an internal WorldArea id.
member.param: internal WorldArea id read from game memory.
resolved display name when found.
member.returns: true if a mapping exists, otherwise false.

member.summary: "normal" or "unique".

member.name: P:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaMeta.Tags
member.summary: Feature tags, e.g. "lineage", "arbiter".

member.name: T:GameHelper.Utils.WorldAreaTags
member.summary:
Maps internal WorldArea MapIds to classification metadata (type, tags).
The mapping is loaded once from the embedded Data/WorldAreaTags.json resource. To refresh, regenerate the JSON file and rebuild.
